questions.question: A researcher studies whether sleep affects task performance. Thirty participants are randomly assigned to sleep either 8 hours or 4 hours the night before testing. Each participant completes one long, cognitively demanding task once, and the outcome is the total time to complete the task. Which statements are correct? (Select all that apply)

options.B: Task completion time is the dependent variable and sleep condition is the predictor
options.D: A paired t-test on participant means is appropriate
options.E: A random intercept for participant should be included
options.F: completion_time ~ condition + (1 + condition | participant) is the most appropriate formula
options.G: A random slope for condition by participant should be included

questions.explanation: This is between-subjects with one observation per participant, so an independent t-test or a regression with sleep condition as the predictor fits, and completion time is the DV. D, E, F and G all assume repeated measures or a mixed model, which is impossible when each participant contributes a single observation in one condition.

questions.question: Which of the following are factors that increase the statistical power of an experiment? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Power (1 - beta) increases with a larger sample, a bigger real effect, less variability, and a larger alpha. E reduces the sample and F adds variability, both of which lower power, so neither belongs.

questions.question: Which statements correctly describe a within-subjects design? (Select all that apply)

options.A: The same participants take part in every condition
options.B: It generally has higher power than a between-subjects design
options.C: Each participant acts as their own control
options.D: It is immune to order effects without any precautions
options.E: It requires roughly twice the sample of a between-subjects design

questions.explanation: In a within-subjects design the same people serve in all conditions, giving each subject as their own control and higher power. D is false because order effects (practice, fatigue, carryover) must be counterbalanced, and E is false because between-subjects needs the larger sample, not within.

questions.question: Which of the following are recognised methods for controlling confounds or demand characteristics? (Select all that apply)

options.D: Adding fillers and a cover story
options.E: Reporting only the p-value of the test
options.F: Letting participants choose their own condition

questions.explanation: Randomisation, counterbalancing, matched stimuli, control conditions, fillers and cover stories all guard against confounds and demand characteristics. E concerns reporting, not control, and F introduces self-selection, which creates rather than removes confounds.

questions.question: A study manipulates background noise (silence vs loud) and task difficulty (easy vs hard) in a 2x2 factorial design. Which statements are correct? (Select all that apply)

options.A: There are four condition combinations in total
options.B: It can only ever detect a single main effect
options.C: It is a correlational design because it has two variables
options.D: Only one independent variable is being manipulated here
options.E: Two independent variables are manipulated at the same time

questions.explanation: A 2x2 factorial crosses two manipulated IVs, yielding four condition combinations. B is false because factorial designs reveal main effects and interactions, C is false because manipulated variables make it experimental, and D is false because two IVs, not one, are manipulated.

questions.question: Which statements about the null hypothesis (H0) and hypothesis testing are correct? (Select all that apply)

options.A: H0 states there is no effect or no difference
options.B: Failing to reject H0 does not prove that H0 is true
options.C: H0 assumes observed differences are due to chance
options.D: A non-significant result conclusively proves H0
options.E: H0 is the directional prediction the researcher hopes to confirm

questions.explanation: H0 is the no-effect assumption attributing observed differences to chance, and a non-significant result only fails to reject it. D is wrong because you can never prove H0, and E describes the alternative hypothesis, not the null.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly pair an experimental design with an appropriate statistical test? (Select all that apply)

options.A: Two between-subjects conditions to an independent t-test
options.B: Two within-subjects conditions to a paired t-test
options.C: A 2x2 factorial design to a factorial ANOVA
options.D: Three within-subjects conditions to an independent t-test
options.E: A single correlation between two measured variables to a paired t-test

questions.explanation: Between-subjects uses an independent t-test, within-subjects uses a paired t-test, and factorial designs use factorial ANOVA. D is wrong because three conditions need (repeated-measures) ANOVA, and E mismatches a correlational question with a paired t-test.

questions.question: Which of the following are commonly cited confounds or sources of bias in behavioural experiments? (Select all that apply)

options.C: Learning or practice effects over trials
options.E: Random assignment of participants to conditions

questions.explanation: Stimulus difficulty, fatigue, learning, demographics and experimenter bias are all classic confounds. E is the opposite: random assignment is a control technique used to neutralise confounds, not a confound itself.

questions.question: A researcher reports that ice-cream consumption is strongly correlated with drowning deaths. Which statements are correct? (Select all that apply)

options.B: A third variable such as summer weather likely drives both
options.C: The correlation alone does not establish that ice cream causes drowning
options.D: It demonstrates a confirmed causal link between the two
options.E: Eliminating ice-cream sales would directly reduce drownings

questions.explanation: This is the textbook spurious correlation, where a third variable (summer) inflates both, so correlation does not imply causation. D and E both wrongly treat the association as a causal mechanism.

questions.question: Which statements about between-subjects designs are correct? (Select all that apply)

options.A: Different participants take part in each condition
options.C: They generally need a larger sample than within designs
options.D: Each participant serves as their own control

questions.explanation: Between-subjects assigns different people to each condition, avoiding order effects but needing roughly twice the sample. D describes a within-subjects feature, and E is wrong because counterbalancing of order applies to within-subjects designs, not between.
